explanation: Ammonium acryloyldimethyltaurate/VP copolymer is a synthetic polymer thickener widely used in lightweight serums and gels, creating a smooth, fluid texture that does not feel heavy or occlusive on skin. It is non-sensitizing, non-comedogenic, and well-tolerated across all skin types including oily and reactive.

Ammonium polyacryloyldimethyl taurate is a synthetic polymer used as a thickener and stabilizer in serums and emulsions, providing a lightweight gel texture without tackiness. It is well-tolerated, non-comedogenic, and suitable for sensitive and oily skin types.

Amodimethicone is an amino-functional silicone that bonds selectively to damaged areas of hair and skin, providing targeted smoothing and conditioning where it is most needed. It is well-tolerated, non-comedogenic, and less likely to cause the buildup or occlusive heaviness associated with standard dimethicone.

Amyl cinnamal is a synthetic jasmine-like fragrance compound classified as a known contact allergen by the EU Scientific Committee and one of 26 fragrance allergens required to be disclosed by name on EU cosmetic labels. It can trigger allergic contact dermatitis, itching, and redness in fragrance-sensitive or reactive individuals, particularly with repeated leave-on exposure.

Ananas sativus (pineapple) fruit extract is a botanical antioxidant containing bromelain enzymes, vitamin C, and flavonoids that provide mild exfoliating, brightening, and free-radical-scavenging benefits. It is generally well-tolerated in rinse-off formulas and at low concentrations in leave-on products, though the enzymatic activity can cause tingling on reactive or barrier-compromised skin.

Ananas sativus fruit extract is a pineapple-derived botanical containing bromelain enzymes and vitamin C that provide mild exfoliating and antioxidant benefits. It is generally well-tolerated at low concentrations, though enzymatic activity may cause tingling on reactive or sensitized skin.

This entry is not a recognized skincare ingredient — it appears to be a product description or label fragment captured during ingredient data collection. It has no functional role in a formula and should be disregarded as a data quality artifact.

Angelica archangelica root extract is a botanical antioxidant containing furanocoumarins, flavonoids, and essential oil compounds that provide free-radical-scavenging and antimicrobial benefits. The furanocoumarin content means it can cause phototoxic reactions on reactive or fair skin when used in leave-on products — it is safer in rinse-off formulas or when sun exposure is avoided after application.

Anhydroxylitol is a sugar-derived humectant that draws moisture to the skin surface and helps stabilize water in the upper skin layers. It is well-tolerated, non-irritating, and is often used alongside glycerin and other humectants to boost the hydrating capacity of a formula.

Aniba rosaeodora (rosewood) wood oil is an essential oil high in linalool — a terpene alcohol classified as a contact sensitizer with repeated exposure. It can trigger allergic contact dermatitis and an itch-and-scratch cycle on reactive or fragrance-sensitive skin, and is particularly problematic in leave-on products.

Anthemis nobilis (Roman chamomile) flower oil is an essential oil used for its calming scent and mild anti-inflammatory properties, but it contains sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds that are recognized contact sensitizers. Despite chamomile's soothing reputation, the concentrated essential oil form can trigger allergic contact dermatitis on reactive or fragrance-sensitive skin.

Aqua is the INCI name for water, the primary solvent in most skincare formulas that dissolves and delivers active ingredients to skin. It is universally well-tolerated and makes up the bulk of most moisturizers, serums, and toners.

Aqua/water/eau is a multilingual listing of water — the primary solvent in most skincare formulas that dissolves and delivers active ingredients to skin. It is universally well-tolerated and makes up the bulk of most moisturizers, serums, and toners.

Arachidyl alcohol is a long-chain C20 fatty alcohol derived from plant or synthetic sources, used as an emollient and thickener that gives creams and lotions a smooth, luxurious texture. It is well-tolerated by sensitive skin — unlike short-chain alcohols, fatty alcohols do not dry or irritate the skin barrier.

Arachidyl glucoside is a mild sugar-based surfactant derived from arachidyl alcohol and glucose, used in gentle cleansers and emulsifiers for its ability to remove impurities without stripping the skin barrier. It is well-tolerated by sensitive skin and is considered a low-irritation alternative to sulfate-based surfactants.

Arbutin (beta-arbutin) is a glycosylated hydroquinone derivative that inhibits tyrosinase — the enzyme responsible for melanin production — to reduce dark spots and even skin tone. It is less potent than alpha-arbutin but similarly well-tolerated by sensitive skin, with a lower risk of irritation than direct hydroquinone.

Argania spinosa (argan) kernel oil is a lightweight, nutrient-rich emollient from the Moroccan argan tree, high in oleic acid, linoleic acid, and tocopherols that support barrier repair and provide antioxidant protection. It absorbs readily, rates low on comedogenicity scales, and is well-tolerated by dry, mature, and combination skin types.

Arginine is a semi-essential amino acid that functions as a natural moisturizing factor (NMF) component in skin, helping to retain moisture and support barrier integrity. It is also used as a pH adjuster and has mild wound-healing properties, making it a well-tolerated and multifunctional addition to hydrating and repairing formulas.

Arginine HCl is the hydrochloride salt form of the amino acid arginine, used interchangeably with arginine as a moisturizing NMF component and mild pH adjuster in skincare formulas. It is well-tolerated and non-irritating across skin types.

Arnica montana flower extract is a botanical with anti-inflammatory sesquiterpene lactones (particularly helenalin) that can reduce bruising and swelling, but is a well-documented contact allergen — particularly for individuals sensitive to the Asteraceae (daisy/ragweed) plant family. It frequently triggers allergic contact dermatitis on reactive skin and should be avoided by anyone with known ragweed or chrysanthemum sensitivities.

Aroma is a catch-all term equivalent to fragrance or parfum, used to label a blend of undisclosed scenting compounds in a formula. Like fragrance, it can contain dozens of sensitizing chemicals that are not individually identified on the label, making it a common trigger for contact dermatitis and an itch-and-scratch cycle on reactive or fragrance-sensitive skin.

Arrowroot (Maranta arundinacea) starch is a fine, naturally derived powder used in skincare as a thickener, oil-absorber, and soft-focus texture agent. It is well-tolerated, non-comedogenic, and particularly useful in lightweight powders and anhydrous formulas for oily or combination skin types.

Artemisia annua (sweet wormwood) extract is a botanical antioxidant containing artemisinin and flavonoids with documented anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It is considered well-tolerated and is used in calming and protective skincare formulas for its ability to reduce oxidative stress and soothe reactive skin.

Artemisia capillaris (capillary wormwood) extract is a traditional East Asian botanical antioxidant rich in scoparone and chlorogenic acid with hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory properties also applicable to skin. It is considered well-tolerated and is used in soothing and brightening formulas for its antioxidant and calming effects.

Artemisia princeps (Japanese mugwort, yomogi) extract is a widely used East Asian botanical antioxidant rich in flavonoids, chlorophyll, and terpenes with soothing, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. It is well-tolerated by most skin types and is particularly valued in K-beauty formulas for calming reactive, sensitized, and acne-prone skin.

Artemisia princeps (Japanese mugwort) leaf extract is a concentrated botanical antioxidant containing flavonoids and terpenes with well-documented anti-inflammatory and skin-calming properties. It is well-tolerated and a key ingredient in many K-beauty formulas targeting reactive, sensitized, and acne-prone skin.

Artemisia princeps (Japanese mugwort) leaf powder is the dried, powdered form of the yomogi plant, used to deliver antioxidant flavonoids and terpenes with anti-inflammatory and soothing benefits in formulas where liquid extracts are not practical. It is well-tolerated and commonly used in K-beauty products for reactive and sensitized skin.

Artemisia vulgaris (common mugwort) extract is a botanical antioxidant containing flavonoids and terpenes with anti-inflammatory properties, used to soothe and protect skin. Note that mugwort is botanically related to ragweed and can cross-react in individuals with ragweed, latex, or certain food allergies — those with known sensitivities should patch-test before use.

Ascorbic acid is the most bioavailable form of vitamin C and a potent antioxidant that neutralizes free radicals, stimulates collagen synthesis, and inhibits melanin production to brighten skin tone. It requires a low pH and airtight packaging to remain stable, and can cause stinging or tingling on reactive or barrier-compromised skin — particularly at concentrations above 15%.

Ascorbyl palmitate is a fat-soluble vitamin C ester formed by combining ascorbic acid with palmitic acid, used as an antioxidant in oil-based formulas and to extend the shelf life of other ingredients. It is more stable than pure ascorbic acid but converts to active vitamin C less efficiently in skin, making it more useful as a formula antioxidant than a potent brightening active.
